paper_title: Achieving vibrational energies of diatomic systems with high quality by machine learning improved DFT method

methods.method_name: Comparison and Analysis of Vibrational Energies
methods.description: This method involves comparing the vibrational energy predictions of diatomic systems using an ANN-improved DFT method against traditional ab initio methods like CCSD(T)/cc-pV5Z to evaluate performance improvements and computational efficiency.

steps.description: The comparison with CCSD(T)/cc-pV5Z results are listed in Table 4.
steps.searchable_summary: compare ANN-improved DFT results with CCSD(T)/cc-pV5Z results

steps.description: As shown in Tables 2 and 4, it can be found that ANN can effectively improve the performance of B3PW91/def2-XVP, even better than the more complex ab initio method (CCSD(T)/cc-pV5Z).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze performance improvement of ANN over B3PW91/def2-XVP and CCSD(T)/cc-pV5Z

steps.description: Some are shown in Fig. 4, the height of red pillar is the average relative deviation of CCSD(T)/cc-pV5Z and the height of blue pillar is the average relative deviation of ANN.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize average relative deviation of CCSD(T)/cc-pV5Z and ANN

steps.description: It shows that the improvement in prediction introduced by ANN over the ab initio method is better than that obtained by expanding the basis set.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate prediction improvement of ANN over basis set expansion

steps.description: Taking Na 35 Cl for an example, it takes more than 40 hours to obtain the results of CCSD(T)/cc-pV5Z, compared to less than one hour for the current method, which includes preparing DFT data and executing the ANN algorithm.
steps.searchable_summary: compare computational time of CCSD(T)/cc-pV5Z and ANN-improved DFT
inputs: computational time data for Na 35 Cl
